title: Artocarpus cuspidatus
taxonomy: Artocarpus cuspidatus is a species of plant in the family Moraceae. This plant is a part of the same family as breadfruit and figs. The Moraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mulberry family. The family Moraceae is native to tropical regions around the world. Members of the Moraceae family are often characterized by their distinctive fruits and leaves.

intro: Artocarpus cuspidatus is a plant species belonging to the Moraceae family, commonly known as the breadfruit tree, although its fruit is not typically used as bread. The plant is known for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the management of conditions such as diabetes, arthritis, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Artocarpus cuspidatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
